title: Hugging Face says it turned to Chinese AI in OpenAI hack
excerpt: Hugging Face used China's GLM 5.2 AI to help with a cyberattack. OpenAI said its models inadvertently caused the breach during tests.
content_html:
The latest OpenAI drama made Chinese AI the hero

By Georgia Hennessy

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Hugging Face said it turned to a Chinese AI model for help after it was hacked by a rogue AI agent. The company behind the intrusion? American AI lab OpenAI.

The incident has become the latest lightning rod for debate in Silicon Valley about the US's approach to regulating powerful AI. Further fanning the flames: Hugging Face said that guardrails prevented it from using American AI in its defense.

For those catching up, here's what you need to know about the saga, what's at stake, and how industry leaders are reacting.

What happened?

Hugging Face, a New York-headquartered platform where developers share and host open AI models and datasets, said in a blog post last week that an attacker had swarmed its systems with tens of thousands of automated actions.

When its security team tried to investigate using an unnamed frontier model, its guardrails blocked it from examining the malicious activity, the company said, because it "cannot distinguish an incident responder from an attacker."

Hugging Face said it switched to GLM 5.2, an open-source model from Beijing-based Z.ai, to analyze more than 17,000 logs the attacker left behind.

On Tuesday, the plot twist arrived. OpenAI said in a blog post that two of its own models, GPT-5.6 Sol and a more capable, unreleased model, autonomously carried out the attack.

OpenAI said the models broke out of a controlled test environment during an internal cybersecurity evaluation, got access to the internet, and hacked into Hugging Face to find answers to the benchmark they were being tested on. Hugging Face said in its blog that it has fixed the vulnerability and is still assessing whether any partner or customer data was affected.

Why are people freaking out?

For tech leaders, the irony was hard to miss: at a moment when Washington is racing to keep American AI ahead of China, a US company under attack from a US AI lab could use Chinese AI tools to help, but not from American providers.

Clement Delangue, CEO of Hugging Face, said in a Wednesday X post that he was "massively grateful" to Z.AI for sharing its open-weights model— meaning developers can inspect, modify, and deploy the model themselves — and added that "it became a key part of our defense."

David Sacks, the cochair of the President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology, said in a Sunday X post that the guardrails on advanced US AI "actually impaired defensive security."

The context: In June, the Trump administration put export controls on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models after reports of a jailbreak in Fable's cyber guardrails, and pressed OpenAI to hold back GPT-5.6 Sol until it could be vetted.

Thomas Wolf, Hugging Face's cofounder and chief scientist, said the incident showed why defenders need open models rather than restricted ones.

"When a frontier model is attacking you and moving laterally inside your infrastructure, defenders need wide access to near-frontier tools within hours or even minutes, rather than being pointed towards a closed-door, vetted application programme for model access," he wrote on X.

This was our first incident of this kind, and we want to thank OpenAI for its transparency about what happened and for the collaboration.

Fortunately, Hugging Face is used to being a target of (human) hackers: we sit at the centre of the AI ecosystem, with all the models,… https://t.co/HJKl4PTNk1

— Thomas Wolf (@Thom_Wolf) July 21, 2026

The Chinese model used by Hugging Face, GLM 5.2, has drawn comparisons to Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.8 and OpenAI's GPT-5.5. It's the latest in a string of buzzy open-source releases from Chinese labs, following Moonshot's Kimi K3 and DeepSeek R1.

How worried should we be about AI hacking?

The incident has startled some in the field, while others are skeptical.

"I'd thought about sci-fi scenarios like this before, but assumed they were at least a couple of years away… and that by then we'd be better prepared, with proper protections in place," Adel Ka, the detection and response lead at Perplexity, wrote on X. "Apparently not. Here we go."

Tom Van de Wiele, an ethical hacker and security advisor, told Business Insider that he is skeptical about some of the claims about the hack and said he is waiting to see more details, such as security logs.

OpenAI called the hack "unprecedented," but it isn't the first AI-driven attack. Anthropic disclosed last November that Chinese state hackers had used Claude to automate most of an espionage campaign, and in July, cybersecurity firm Sysdig documented AI-assisted ransomware.

In both cases, humans directed the targets. The Hugging Face breach appears to be the first with no human in the loop at all.

OpenAI has since added Hugging Face to its "trusted access" program, which gives it a version of GPT-5.6 Sol with fewer cyber restrictions for defensive use.

Raghu Nandakumara, the VP of industry strategy at security firm Illumio, told Business Insider that powerful AI going rogue was foreseeable.

"AI guardrails were never designed to be security boundaries. They're there to influence behavior, not guarantee it," he said.

Read next

Georgia Hennessy

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Georgia is a fellow at Business Insider's London office.Before joining Business Insider, she worked at Japan's largest newspaper, The Yomiuri Shimbun, and interned at the Financial Times. She is an NCTJ-qualified journalist with a degree in Philosophy from the University of Birmingham. You can contact her via email at ghennessy@insider.com

  • Artificial Intelligence
  • China

  • Cybersecurity
  • Innovation

blocks.html_en: What happened?

sentences.en: What happened?

blocks.html_en: Hugging Face, a New York-headquartered platform where developers share and host open AI models and datasets, said in a blog post last week that an attacker had swarmed its systems with tens of thousands of automated actions.

sentences.en: Hugging Face, a New York-headquartered platform where developers share and host open AI models and datasets, said in a blog post last week that an attacker had swarmed its systems with tens of thousands of automated actions.
sentences.zh: Hugging Face 是一家总部位于纽约的平台，开发人员在该平台上共享和托管开放式人工智能模型和数据集。该平台上周在一篇博客文章中表示，攻击者已通过数万个自动化操作席卷了其系统。

blocks.html_en: When its security team tried to investigate using an unnamed frontier model, its guardrails blocked it from examining the malicious activity, the company said, because it "cannot distinguish an incident responder from an attacker."

sentences.en: When its security team tried to investigate using an unnamed frontier model, its guardrails blocked it from examining the malicious activity, the company said, because it "cannot distinguish an incident responder from an attacker."
sentences.zh: 该公司表示，当其安全团队试图使用未命名的前沿模型进行调查时，其护栏阻止了其检查恶意活动，因为它“无法区分事件响应者和攻击者”。

blocks.html_en: Hugging Face said it switched to GLM 5.2, an open-source model from Beijing-based Z.ai, to analyze more than 17,000 logs the attacker left behind.

sentences.en: Hugging Face said it switched to GLM 5.2, an open-source model from Beijing-based Z.ai, to analyze more than 17,000 logs the attacker left behind.
sentences.zh: Hugging Face 表示，它改用了 GLM 5.2（北京 Z.ai 的开源模型）来分析攻击者留下的 17,000 多条日志。

blocks.html_en: On Tuesday, the plot twist arrived. OpenAI said in a blog post that two of its own models, GPT-5.6 Sol and a more capable, unreleased model, autonomously carried out the attack.

sentences.en: On Tuesday, the plot twist arrived.
sentences.zh: 周二，剧情出现转折。

sentences.en: OpenAI said in a blog post that two of its own models, GPT-5.6 Sol and a more capable, unreleased model, autonomously carried out the attack.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 在一篇博客文章中表示，其自己的两个模型 GPT-5.6 Sol 和一个功能更强大、未发布的模型自主执行了此次攻击。

blocks.html_en: OpenAI said the models broke out of a controlled test environment during an internal cybersecurity evaluation, got access to the internet, and hacked into Hugging Face to find answers to the benchmark they were being tested on. Hugging Face said in its blog that it has fixed the vulnerability and is still assessing whether any partner or customer data was affected.

sentences.en: OpenAI said the models broke out of a controlled test environment during an internal cybersecurity evaluation, got access to the internet, and hacked into Hugging Face to find answers to the benchmark they were being tested on.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 表示，这些模型在内部网络安全评估期间突破了受控测试环境，访问了互联网，并侵入了 Hugging Face，以找到正在测试的基准的答案。

sentences.en: Hugging Face said in its blog that it has fixed the vulnerability and is still assessing whether any partner or customer data was affected.
sentences.zh: Hugging Face 在其博客中表示，它已经修复了该漏洞，并且仍在评估是否有任何合作伙伴或客户数据受到影响。

blocks.html_en: Why are people freaking out?

sentences.en: Why are people freaking out?
sentences.zh: 为什么人们会惊慌失措？

blocks.html_en: For tech leaders, the irony was hard to miss: at a moment when Washington is racing to keep American AI ahead of China, a US company under attack from a US AI lab could use Chinese AI tools to help, but not from American providers.

sentences.en: For tech leaders, the irony was hard to miss: at a moment when Washington is racing to keep American AI ahead of China, a US company under attack from a US AI lab could use Chinese AI tools to help, but not from American providers.
sentences.zh: 对于科技领导者来说，具有讽刺意味的是：在华盛顿竞相让美国人工智能领先于中国的时刻，一家受到美国人工智能实验室攻击的美国公司可以使用中国的人工智能工具来提供帮助，但不能使用美国提供商的工具。

blocks.html_en: Clement Delangue, CEO of Hugging Face, said in a Wednesday X post that he was "massively grateful" to Z.AI for sharing its open-weights model— meaning developers can inspect, modify, and deploy the model themselves — and added that "it became a key part of our defense."

sentences.en: Clement Delangue, CEO of Hugging Face, said in a Wednesday X post that he was "massively grateful" to Z.AI for sharing its open-weights model— meaning developers can inspect, modify, and deploy the model themselves — and added that "it became a key part of our defense."
sentences.zh: Hugging Face 首席执行官 Clement Delangue 在周三的一篇帖子中表示，他“非常感谢”Z.AI 分享其开放权重模型——这意味着开发人员可以自己检查、修改和部署该模型——并补充说“它成为我们防御的关键部分。”

blocks.html_en: David Sacks, the cochair of the President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology, said in a Sunday X post that the guardrails on advanced US AI "actually impaired defensive security."

sentences.en: David Sacks, the cochair of the President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology, said in a Sunday X post that the guardrails on advanced US AI "actually impaired defensive security."
sentences.zh: 总统科学技术顾问委员会联合主席戴维·萨克斯 (David Sacks) 在 Sunday X 的一篇文章中表示，美国先进人工智能的护栏“实际上损害了防御安全”。

blocks.html_en: The context: In June, the Trump administration put export controls on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models after reports of a jailbreak in Fable's cyber guardrails, and pressed OpenAI to hold back GPT-5.6 Sol until it could be vetted.

sentences.en: The context: In June, the Trump administration put export controls on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models after reports of a jailbreak in Fable's cyber guardrails, and pressed OpenAI to hold back GPT-5.6 Sol until it could be vetted.
sentences.zh: 背景：今年 6 月，在有报道称 Fable 的网络护栏越狱后，特朗普政府对 Anthropic 的 Fable 5 和 Mythos 5 模型实施了出口管制，并敦促 OpenAI 暂缓 GPT-5.6 Sol 直至接受审查。

blocks.html_en: Thomas Wolf, Hugging Face's cofounder and chief scientist, said the incident showed why defenders need open models rather than restricted ones.

sentences.zh: Hugging Face 联合创始人兼首席科学家托马斯·沃尔夫 (Thomas Wolf) 表示，这一事件表明了为什么防御者需要开放模型而不是受限模型。

blocks.html_en: "When a frontier model is attacking you and moving laterally inside your infrastructure, defenders need wide access to near-frontier tools within hours or even minutes, rather than being pointed towards a closed-door, vetted application programme for model access," he wrote on X.

sentences.en: "When a frontier model is attacking you and moving laterally inside your infrastructure, defenders need wide access to near-frontier tools within hours or even minutes, rather than being pointed towards a closed-door, vetted application programme for model access," he wrote on X.
sentences.zh: 他在 X 上写道：“当前沿模型攻击你并在你的基础设施内部横向移动时，防御者需要在几小时甚至几分钟内广泛访问近前沿工具，而不是被指向一个闭门的、经过审查的模型访问应用程序。”

sentences.zh: OpenAI 称此次黑客攻击“史无前例”，但这并不是第一次由人工智能驱动的攻击。

sentences.en: Anthropic disclosed last November that Chinese state hackers had used Claude to automate most of an espionage campaign, and in July, cybersecurity firm Sysdig documented AI-assisted ransomware.
sentences.zh: Anthropic 去年 11 月披露，中国国家黑客利用 Claude 自动化了大部分间谍活动，7 月，网络安全公司 Sysdig 记录了人工智能辅助勒索软件。

blocks.html_en: In both cases, humans directed the targets. The Hugging Face breach appears to be the first with no human in the loop at all.

sentences.en: In both cases, humans directed the targets.
sentences.zh: 在这两种情况下，都是人类引导目标。

sentences.en: The Hugging Face breach appears to be the first with no human in the loop at all.
sentences.zh: “拥抱脸”漏洞似乎是第一个完全没有人参与的漏洞。

blocks.html_en: OpenAI has since added Hugging Face to its "trusted access" program, which gives it a version of GPT-5.6 Sol with fewer cyber restrictions for defensive use.

sentences.en: OpenAI has since added Hugging Face to its "trusted access" program, which gives it a version of GPT-5.6 Sol with fewer cyber restrictions for defensive use.
sentences.zh: 此后，OpenAI 将 Hugging Face 添加到其“可信访问”计划中，这为其提供了一个 GPT-5.6 Sol 版本，用于防御用途的网络限制较少。

blocks.html_en: Raghu Nandakumara, the VP of industry strategy at security firm Illumio, told Business Insider that powerful AI going rogue was foreseeable.

sentences.en: Raghu Nandakumara, the VP of industry strategy at security firm Illumio, told Business Insider that powerful AI going rogue was foreseeable.
sentences.zh: 安全公司 Illumio 的行业战略副总裁 Raghu Nandakumara 告诉《商业内幕》，强大的人工智能变得流氓是可以预见的。

blocks.html_en: "AI guardrails were never designed to be security boundaries. They're there to influence behavior, not guarantee it," he said.

sentences.en: "AI guardrails were never designed to be security boundaries.
sentences.zh: “人工智能护栏从来就不是为了安全边界而设计的。

sentences.en: They're there to influence behavior, not guarantee it," he said.
sentences.zh: 他们的作用是影响行为，而不是保证行为，”他说。

content_html_zh:
OpenAI最新剧让中国AI成为英雄

作者：Georgia Hennessy 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

Hugging Face 表示，在遭到流氓人工智能代理的黑客攻击后，它向中国人工智能模型寻求帮助。 入侵背后的公司？ 美国人工智能实验室OpenAI。

该事件已成为硅谷关于美国监管强大人工智能方法的争论的最新热点。 进一步煽风点火：Hugging Face 表示，护栏阻止其使用美国人工智能进行防御。

对于那些追赶潮流的人来说，以下是您需要了解的关于这一事件的信息、其中的利害关系以及行业领导者的反应。

发生了什么？

Hugging Face 是一家总部位于纽约的平台，开发人员在该平台上共享和托管开放式人工智能模型和数据集。该平台上周在一篇博客文章中表示，攻击者已通过数万个自动化操作席卷了其系统。

该公司表示，当其安全团队试图使用未命名的前沿模型进行调查时，其护栏阻止了其检查恶意活动，因为它“无法区分事件响应者和攻击者”。

Hugging Face 表示，它改用了 GLM 5.2（北京 Z.ai 的开源模型）来分析攻击者留下的 17,000 多条日志。

周二，剧情出现转折。 OpenAI 在一篇博客文章中表示，其自己的两个模型 GPT-5.6 Sol 和一个功能更强大、未发布的模型自主执行了此次攻击。

OpenAI 表示，这些模型在内部网络安全评估期间突破了受控测试环境，访问了互联网，并侵入了 Hugging Face，以找到正在测试的基准的答案。 Hugging Face 在其博客中表示，它已经修复了该漏洞，并且仍在评估是否有任何合作伙伴或客户数据受到影响。

为什么人们会惊慌失措？

对于科技领导者来说，具有讽刺意味的是：在华盛顿竞相让美国人工智能领先于中国的时刻，一家受到美国人工智能实验室攻击的美国公司可以使用中国的人工智能工具来提供帮助，但不能使用美国提供商的工具。

Hugging Face 首席执行官 Clement Delangue 在周三的一篇帖子中表示，他“非常感谢”Z.AI 分享其开放权重模型——这意味着开发人员可以自己检查、修改和部署该模型——并补充说“它成为我们防御的关键部分。”

总统科学技术顾问委员会联合主席戴维·萨克斯 (David Sacks) 在 Sunday X 的一篇文章中表示，美国先进人工智能的护栏“实际上损害了防御安全”。

背景：今年 6 月，在有报道称 Fable 的网络护栏越狱后，特朗普政府对 Anthropic 的 Fable 5 和 Mythos 5 模型实施了出口管制，并敦促 OpenAI 暂缓 GPT-5.6 Sol 直至接受审查。

Hugging Face 联合创始人兼首席科学家托马斯·沃尔夫 (Thomas Wolf) 表示，这一事件表明了为什么防御者需要开放模型而不是受限模型。

他在 X 上写道：“当前沿模型攻击你并在你的基础设施内部横向移动时，防御者需要在几小时甚至几分钟内广泛访问近前沿工具，而不是被指向一个闭门的、经过审查的模型访问应用程序。”

这是我们第一次发生此类事件，我们要感谢 OpenAI 对所发生事件的透明度以及合作。幸运的是，Hugging Face 习惯于成为（人类）黑客的目标：我们位于人工智能生态系统的中心，拥有所有模型，…… https://t.co/HJKl4PTNk1—托马斯·沃尔夫 (@Thom_Wolf) 2026 年 7 月 21 日

Hugging Face 使用的中国模型 GLM 5.2 与 Anthropic 的 Claude Opus 4.8 和 OpenAI 的 GPT-5.5 进行了比较。 这是继 Moonshot 的 Kimi K3 和 DeepSeek R1 之后，中国实验室发布的一系列热门开源版本中的最新版本。

我们应该对人工智能黑客攻击有多担心？

这一事件让一些业内人士感到震惊，而另一些人则表示怀疑。

“我以前曾想过这样的科幻场景，但假设它们至少还需要几年的时间...... 到那时我们就会做好更好的准备，采取适当的保护措施，”Perplexity 的检测和响应负责人阿德尔·卡 (Adel Ka) 在 X 上写道。 “显然不是。 开始了。”

道德黑客兼安全顾问 Tom Van de Wiele 告诉《商业内幕》，他对一些有关此次黑客攻击的说法持怀疑态度，并表示他正在等待查看更多细节，例如安全日志。

OpenAI 称此次黑客攻击“史无前例”，但这并不是第一次由人工智能驱动的攻击。 Anthropic 去年 11 月披露，中国国家黑客利用 Claude 自动化了大部分间谍活动，7 月，网络安全公司 Sysdig 记录了人工智能辅助勒索软件。

在这两种情况下，都是人类引导目标。 “拥抱脸”漏洞似乎是第一个完全没有人参与的漏洞。

此后，OpenAI 将 Hugging Face 添加到其“可信访问”计划中，这为其提供了一个 GPT-5.6 Sol 版本，用于防御用途的网络限制较少。

安全公司 Illumio 的行业战略副总裁 Raghu Nandakumara 告诉《商业内幕》，强大的人工智能变得流氓是可以预见的。

“人工智能护栏从来就不是为了安全边界而设计的。 他们的作用是影响行为，而不是保证行为，”他说。

阅读下一篇

Georgia Hennessy 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。
